meta.title: Careersy Content Generation System — Creator Knowledge Graph
meta.description: Cross-creator analysis of 6 LinkedIn career content creators. First layer for the Careersy content generation system.

creators.name: Adam Broda
creators.voice: Confident, data-driven, coach-authority. Uses bold claims backed by market data.
creators.positioning: The career cheat-code guy for $150K-$350K roles
creators.headline: Career Coach | Helping Professionals Land $150K-$350K Roles
creators.core_thesis: The job market is rigged — but I'll show you the cheat codes

creators.name: Jan Tegze
creators.voice: Recruiter-candour, insider tone, educational. European pragmatism.
creators.positioning: The recruiter who tells candidates what recruiters won't
creators.headline: Director of Talent Acquisition @ Tricentis | Author | Speaker
creators.core_thesis: I'm a recruiter telling you what recruiters won't tell you

creators.name: Reno Perry
creators.voice: Authority, direct, system-oriented. Every post is a mini-framework.
creators.headline: Career Coach | Job Search Strategist
creators.core_thesis: 6-figure offers don't go to the best candidates — they go to the best-prepared

creators.name: Bonnie Dilber
creators.voice: Casual, direct, educator-instincts. Uses 'y'all'. Treats audience as peers.
creators.positioning: The recruiter who tells you what HR doesn't want you to know
creators.headline: Recruiting Leader @ Zapier | Former Educator
creators.core_thesis: I'll tell you the truth about recruiting that HR doesn't want you to know

creators.name: Austin Belcak
creators.voice: Confident, systematic, coach-like. Everything is a framework or script.
creators.positioning: The anti-application guy — get hired without applying online
creators.headline: Founder @ Cultivated Culture | I Teach People How To Land Amazing Jobs Without Applying Online

creators.name: Lee Harding
creators.voice: Sharp, funny, no-nonsense. British directness. Anti-guru energy.
creators.positioning: The honest introverted recruiter who doesn't sugarcoat it
creators.headline: The Introverted Recruiter | Talent Acquisition Director @ Join Talent
creators.core_thesis: Most job search advice is rubbish — here's what actually happens

topics.name: Job Search Tactics & Systems
topics.description: Practical frameworks for running an effective job search: applications, tracking, follow-ups, timing

topics.name: Resume & LinkedIn Optimization
topics.description: Profile optimization, headline writing, resume formatting, ATS strategies

topics.name: Networking & Cold Outreach
topics.description: Referral strategies, cold emails, informational interviews, building connections

topics.name: Labor Market Data & News
topics.description: Job market statistics, hiring trends, layoffs, economic data commentary

topics.name: AI & Future of Work
topics.description: AI tools for job search, AI's impact on hiring, building AI skills, AI replacing jobs

topics.name: Recruiting Insider Truths
topics.description: Behind-the-curtain recruiting processes, what recruiters actually look at, hiring biases

topics.name: Interview Preparation
topics.description: Behavioral questions, STAR method, negotiation tactics, follow-up emails

topics.name: Salary Negotiation
topics.description: Negotiation scripts, benchmark data, counter-offer strategies, total compensation

topics.name: Personal Branding & Visibility
topics.description: Building LinkedIn presence, thought leadership, portfolio strategy, content creation for job seekers

topics.name: Mindset & Motivation
topics.description: Overcoming rejection, imposter syndrome, career confidence, persistence

topics.name: Social Proof & Case Studies
topics.description: Client success stories, testimonial screenshots, before/after career transformations

topics.name: CV Screening & ATS Reality
topics.description: How CVs are actually reviewed, ATS myths debunked, what gets past the first filter

templates.name: Data/News Hook
templates.structure: Lead with specific stat or news event → Add insider perspective → CTA
templates.why_it_works: Combines timeliness with authority. Reader gets news + expert interpretation in one post.
templates.example_opener: The job market lost another 92k jobs in February...

templates.name: Numbered List / Micro-Guide
templates.structure: Problem headline → 3-7 numbered actionable items → Each with copy-paste script/template → CTA
templates.why_it_works: Maximum actionability per post. Readers can implement same-day. High save/bookmark rate.
templates.example_opener: 5 Steps To A Job-Winning LinkedIn Headline:

templates.name: Contrarian Reframe
templates.structure: Hot take against consensus → Invite disagreement → Numbered reasoning → CTA
templates.why_it_works: Pattern interrupt. Generates debate in comments. Algorithm rewards controversy.
templates.example_opener: I don't really buy that companies are laying people off 'because of AI'...

templates.name: Insider Reveal
templates.structure: "Here's what actually happens when..." → Step-by-step behind-the-scenes → Actionable takeaway
templates.why_it_works: Information asymmetry is powerful. Candidates desperately want to know what happens on the other side.
templates.example_opener: I've reviewed 3,000+ resumes this past year...

templates.name: Social Proof Story
templates.structure: Screenshot of client DM/email/result → Brief context → "Here's what they did" → CTA
templates.why_it_works: Social proof is the most persuasive format. Shows real results, not theory.
templates.example_opener: Just got this message from a client who landed at Google...

templates.name: Build-in-Public Diary
templates.structure: Personal action taken → Tag companies/people → Share learning → Invite others to join
templates.why_it_works: Makes complex topics approachable. Positions creator as relatable learner, not distant expert.
templates.example_opener: I built my first skill in Claude Code yesterday!

templates.name: Myth-Buster
templates.structure: Name common belief → Explain why it's wrong from experience → Give actual reality → CTA
templates.why_it_works: Frustration-driven engagement. People WANT to believe the system is broken and share when validated.
templates.example_opener: You don't need to 'beat the ATS'...

templates.name: Authority Opener
templates.structure: Credential/experience claim → Bold statement → Evidence/framework → CTA
templates.why_it_works: Establishes trust immediately. Reader knows why they should listen before the advice begins.
templates.example_opener: I've coached 500+ job seekers this year. Here's what separates the ones who land offers...

monetization_models.name: High-Ticket Coaching
monetization_models.description: Free content → book a call → discovery session → 1:1 or group coaching packages. High revenue per client, limited scale.
monetization_models.conversion_path: Post → CTA → Typeform/Calendly → Discovery Call → Enrollment

monetization_models.name: Low-Ticket Digital Products
monetization_models.description: Free content → email list → self-serve courses, tools, templates. High volume, heavily automated. Scales without creator time.
monetization_models.conversion_path: Post → Free Resource → Email Sequence → Product Purchase

monetization_models.name: Brand Partnerships
monetization_models.description: Build large audience → brands pay for sponsored content, integrations, endorsements. Creator doesn't sell to audience directly.
monetization_models.conversion_path: Content → Audience Growth → Brand Deal Inquiry → Sponsored Post

ctas.name: Book an Appointment
ctas.mechanism: Typeform/Calendly link in every post and bio
ctas.placement: End of post + comments + profile header

ctas.name: Download Free Resource
ctas.placement: End of post with specific resource name

ctas.name: Linktree Hub
ctas.mechanism: Single link aggregating all offers, newsletter, social links

ctas.name: Comment-Triggered Auto-DM
ctas.mechanism: "Comment [KEYWORD] and I'll send you the guide" → StanAutoDM delivers resource

ctas.name: Branded Infographic CTA
ctas.mechanism: Infographic with creator branding includes CTA as last slide or watermark

patterns.name: Own ONE Contrarian Idea
patterns.description: Every top creator owns a single contrarian thesis that all content flows from. This idea is repeatable, ownable, and instantly recognizable.
patterns.careersy_implication: Eli needs ONE idea: 'The ANZ job market doesn't reward the best candidates — it punishes the ones who don't know the game.' Maps to the Invisible Applicant avatar.

patterns.name: Five Universal Content Templates
patterns.description: Data Hook, Numbered List, Contrarian Reframe, Insider Reveal, Social Proof Story — every creator uses 3-5 of these in rotation.
patterns.careersy_implication: Start with Contrarian Reframe and Insider Reveal — they map directly to Careersy audience language from intake calls.

patterns.name: Three Monetization Models
patterns.description: High-ticket coaching (Broda, Perry), low-ticket digital products (Belcak, Harding), brand partnerships (Dilber). Each requires different content volume and audience size.
patterns.careersy_implication: Careersy runs coaching model. Add a $49-$97 AUD tripwire product before coaching upsell to address price sensitivity from intake calls.

patterns.name: Standardized CTA on Every Post
patterns.description: All 6 creators have ONE consistent CTA on every single post. No post goes without a conversion mechanism.
patterns.careersy_implication: Standardize: 'Comment AUDIT and I'll send you my ANZ resume scorecard' — combines Harding's auto-DM with Perry's booking endgame.

patterns.name: 80/15/5 Value-to-Sell Ratio
patterns.description: 80% pure value posts, 15% soft-sell (case studies, testimonials), 5% direct-sell (launches, enrollment). Nobody leads with the sell.
patterns.careersy_implication: Use 91 testimonials as Social Proof Story factory — one per week. Remaining posts are pure value.

patterns.name: Text-First, Visuals as Scroll-Stoppers
patterns.description: Text-only posts dominate for engagement. Branded infographics and carousels used as scroll-stoppers. Video growing but optional.
patterns.careersy_implication: Start with text-only posts (lowest production cost). Add one branded template for infographic posts. Video optional.

style_profiles.name: Adam Broda Style
style_profiles.punchline_style: Bold declarative closer. Single punchy sentence as its own paragraph. Often starts with 'The truth is:' or 'Stop doing this.'
style_profiles.metaphor_style: Gaming/competition metaphors. Job market as a rigged game with cheat codes and hidden rules.

style_profiles.name: Jan Tegze Style
style_profiles.punchline_style: Educational closer. Ends with a practical takeaway or a reframe of the conventional wisdom. Measured, not dramatic.
style_profiles.metaphor_style: Behind-the-curtain metaphors. Recruiting as a hidden process with insider knowledge.

style_profiles.name: Reno Perry Style
style_profiles.punchline_style: Framework-ending authority statement. Posts conclude with a system or repeatable process. The closer is always actionable, never philosophical.
signature_phrases: Most candidates do X. Top candidates do Y.
style_profiles.metaphor_style: Military/sports preparation metaphors. Job search as a campaign that rewards the best-prepared, not the best-qualified.

style_profiles.name: Bonnie Dilber Style
style_profiles.punchline_style: Invitational closer. Ends with a question to the audience or an invitation to share their experience. Builds community, not authority.
style_profiles.metaphor_style: Classroom/learning metaphors. Recruiting as a learning process where everyone is figuring it out together.

style_profiles.name: Austin Belcak Style
style_profiles.punchline_style: Conversion closer. Every post ends with a clear next step — download this, get the guide, follow for more. The punchline IS the CTA.
signature_phrases: I used this to land a job at Google/Microsoft/etc.
Most people do X. Do this instead:
style_profiles.metaphor_style: Hacking/shortcut metaphors. Job search as a system to be hacked with the right scripts and templates.

style_profiles.name: Lee Harding Style
style_profiles.punchline_style: Mic-drop closer. Short, punchy final line that reframes everything. Often sarcastic or blunt. Designed to be screenshot-worthy.
signature_phrases: The truth nobody wants to hear:
Comment CV and I'll send you the guide.
style_profiles.metaphor_style: Debunking/myth-busting metaphors. Job search advice industry as snake oil that needs exposing.

hook_types.name: Contrarian Statement
hook_types.mechanism: Opens with a bold claim that contradicts common wisdom. Triggers cognitive dissonance — reader MUST keep reading to resolve the tension.
hook_types.structure: [Bold claim against consensus]. Here's why: / But here's the truth:
example_openers: I don't really buy that companies are laying people off 'because of AI'.
Most job search advice is rubbish.

hook_types.name: Data/Stat Shock
hook_types.mechanism: Leads with a surprising, specific number that creates urgency or challenges assumptions. The specificity signals authority.
hook_types.structure: [Specific number/stat that surprises]. Here's what this means for you:
example_openers: The job market lost another 92k jobs in February.
I've reviewed 3,000+ resumes this past year. Only 12 made it to the final round.
97% of resumes get rejected before a human ever sees them.

hook_types.name: Curiosity Gap
hook_types.mechanism: Opens an information loop that can only be closed by reading the full post. Creates cognitive tension via the Zeigarnik effect.
hook_types.structure: [Set up an incomplete scenario]. Here's what they all had in common: / Here's what happened next:
example_openers: I've coached 500+ job seekers this year. Here's what separates the ones who land offers:
I asked 50 hiring managers one question. Their answers will change how you job search.
There's one thing every 6-figure candidate does that nobody talks about.

hook_types.name: Problem Statement
hook_types.mechanism: Names the reader's pain directly. Reader self-selects in because they feel seen. Activates the 'this is about me' response.
hook_types.structure: If you're [experiencing pain], here's what you need to know: / If you're [in situation], stop [common mistake].
example_openers: If you're like me and you were confused about this concept of dogfooding your own product.
If you've been job searching for 3+ months with no interviews, your resume isn't the problem.
You're not getting ghosted because you're unqualified. You're getting ghosted because of this:

hook_types.name: Personal Confession
hook_types.mechanism: Opens with vulnerability or a personal admission. Breaks the expert-audience barrier. Creates relatability and trust simultaneously.
hook_types.structure: I [did something unexpected / failed at something / changed my mind]. Here's what I learned:
example_openers: I built my first skill in Claude Code yesterday!
I used to think networking was manipulative. I was wrong.
I almost quit my career coaching business last year. Here's why I stayed:

hook_types.name: List Promise
hook_types.mechanism: Promises a specific number of actionable items. Reader knows exactly what they'll get. The number creates a contract — 'read this, get X things.'
example_openers: 5 Steps To A Job-Winning LinkedIn Headline:
7 resume mistakes that get you instantly rejected:
3 cold email scripts that actually get responses:

content_frameworks.name: Hook-Problem-Insight-Action
content_frameworks.structure: Hook (1-2 lines) -> Problem statement (2-3 lines) -> Insight/reframe (3-5 lines) -> Actionable steps (numbered) -> CTA
content_frameworks.best_for: Job search tactics, resume optimization, networking strategies — any topic where the reader needs both understanding and next steps
content_frameworks.engagement_pattern: High save rate. Readers bookmark for the action steps. Comments often ask follow-up questions.

content_frameworks.name: Hook-List-CTA
content_frameworks.structure: Hook line (1 line) -> Numbered list of 3-7 items (each 1-3 lines with copy-paste templates) -> CTA
content_frameworks.best_for: Scripts, templates, step-by-step guides — anything readers can implement immediately
content_frameworks.engagement_pattern: Highest save rate of all frameworks. Lower comment count but massive reshare value.

content_frameworks.name: Claim-Evidence-Reframe
content_frameworks.structure: Bold contrarian claim (1-2 lines) -> Evidence/reasoning (3-5 numbered points) -> Reframe what reader should do instead -> CTA
content_frameworks.best_for: Myth-busting, challenging conventional wisdom, positioning against competitors
content_frameworks.engagement_pattern: Highest comment rate. Generates debate and disagreement. Algorithm rewards the controversy.

content_frameworks.name: Story-Lesson-System
content_frameworks.structure: Personal/client story (3-5 lines) -> Key lesson extracted (2-3 lines) -> Systematic takeaway (numbered) -> CTA
content_frameworks.best_for: Case studies, social proof, personal experience posts — anything where narrative drives the insight
content_frameworks.engagement_pattern: High engagement across all metrics. Story creates emotional investment, system creates practical value.

content_frameworks.name: Question-Stack-Resolution
content_frameworks.structure: Provocative question (1 line) -> Stack of related questions building tension (3-5 questions) -> Resolution/answer -> CTA
content_frameworks.best_for: Engagement-first posts, thought leadership, opinion pieces — when you want maximum comments
content_frameworks.engagement_pattern: Very high comment rate. Questions invite responses. Works especially well for recruiting transparency topics.

engagement_techniques.name: Curiosity Gap
engagement_techniques.mechanism: Opens an information loop in the reader's mind that can only be closed by reading further. Leverages the Zeigarnik effect.
engagement_techniques.placement: Hook line and section transitions. Most effective as the very first sentence.
engagement_techniques.example: I've reviewed 3,000+ resumes this past year. Only 12 made it to the final round. Here's what they all had in common:
engagement_techniques.psychological_driver: Zeigarnik effect — incomplete information creates cognitive tension that demands resolution.

engagement_techniques.name: Pattern Interrupt
engagement_techniques.mechanism: Breaks the expected flow of content with an unexpected statement, question, or format change. Resets the reader's attention clock.
engagement_techniques.placement: Mid-post, typically after 3-4 paragraphs when attention naturally wanes. Also used as opening hook.
engagement_techniques.example: Wait. Read that again. (Used after a key insight to force re-reading.)
engagement_techniques.psychological_driver: Orienting response — novel stimuli trigger automatic attention refocusing.

engagement_techniques.name: Ultra-Short Paragraph Pacing
engagement_techniques.mechanism: Every paragraph is 1-3 lines maximum. Single-sentence paragraphs used for emphasis. Creates rapid visual flow that mimics conversation speed.
engagement_techniques.placement: Throughout the entire post. This is a structural rule, not a technique applied at specific moments.
engagement_techniques.example:
This is the secret.

Nobody will tell you this.

But the job market doesn't reward the best candidates.

It rewards the ones who know how to play the game.
engagement_techniques.psychological_driver: Cognitive fluency — short paragraphs reduce perceived effort, making the content feel easier and faster to consume.

engagement_techniques.name: Direct Address / 'You' Language
engagement_techniques.mechanism: Speaks directly to the reader using 'you', 'your', and imperative verbs. Creates a 1:1 conversation feeling even in a broadcast medium.
engagement_techniques.placement: Every 2-3 sentences. Especially in hook lines, action steps, and CTAs.
engagement_techniques.example: You're sending 200 applications a month. You're getting zero callbacks. Here's why: your resume is optimized for ATS, not for humans.
engagement_techniques.psychological_driver: Self-referencing effect — people process information more deeply when it feels personally directed at them.

engagement_techniques.name: Open Loop / Cliffhanger
engagement_techniques.mechanism: Teases upcoming information without delivering it immediately. Creates forward momentum that pulls the reader through the post.
engagement_techniques.placement: After the hook (before the main content) and before the CTA (to motivate clicking through).
engagement_techniques.example: But here's what nobody realizes... (followed by 2-3 paragraphs of build-up before the actual reveal)
engagement_techniques.psychological_driver: Information gap theory — the discomfort of not-knowing motivates continued engagement.

engagement_techniques.name: Rhetorical Question Stack
engagement_techniques.mechanism: Asks 2-4 questions the reader already knows the answer to. Creates a 'yes-ladder' that builds agreement before the main point.
engagement_techniques.placement: Opening hook or mid-post transition. Most effective when stacked in groups of 3.
engagement_techniques.example: Are you tired of applying to 50+ jobs with no response? Do you wonder why you never hear back? Have you considered that your resume might not be the problem?
engagement_techniques.psychological_driver: Consistency principle — once readers mentally say 'yes' to initial questions, they're more likely to agree with the conclusion.

edges.conversion_path: Viral debate → massive reach → brand value
Insider knowledge → trust → want personalized help
Debunk myth → show correct approach → sell the tool
